rawOperations.description: Create multiple DNS records in a single transaction. Requires domain ownership. All records will be validated together against DNS zone rules.
Delete multiple DNS records by their IDs in a single transaction. Requires domain ownership. The zone will be validated after deletion.
Update multiple DNS records in a single transaction. Requires domain ownership. All records must belong to the same zone and will be validated together.
Check if a domain is available for registration. Returns availability status, pricing details, current owner (if any), and registration constraints.
Create a new DNS record for a domain. Requires domain ownership. The record will be validated against DNS zone rules before creation.
Delete a DNS record by its ID. Requires domain ownership. The deletion will be validated to ensure zone integrity.
Return every registered EIP-712 struct type used by the Namefi API.
Retrieve the list of blockchain networks that are allowed for SIWE authentication.

Retrieve NFSC (Namefi Stable Coin) balance across all supported blockchain chains for the authenticated user's linked wallet addresses. Returns both raw contract values and human-readable USD amounts.
Retrieve all DNS records for a specified domain zone. Returns an array of DNS records including A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, and other record types.
Retrieve a list of domains or parent domains(ie; their subdomains are available for trial)
Return the EIP-712 domain separator used for Namefi payload signing. The Namefi domain is chain-agnostic (chainId omitted) to allow cross-chain signatures.

rawOperations.summary: Get EIP-712 types for a method
rawOperations.description: Return the accepted primary types and full type map for a specific API operation that supports EIP-712 authentication.
Retrieve detailed information about an order including items, payments, and user details. User must own the order.
Retrieve all order items for the current user, sorted by creation date in descending order.
Retrieve payment method details for an order, including card info for Stripe payments and wallet addresses for on-chain payments.
Generate and store a replay-protected nonce for a wallet address before signing a SIWE message.
Generate domain suggestions based on a search query. Supports pagination and optional parent domain filtering for third-level domain suggestions.
Retrieve all domains owned by the current user. Returns domain details including DNS status, nameservers, and expiration dates.
Retrieve all order items for the current user. Returns order items sorted by creation date in descending order.
Check whether a domain has the standard parking DNS records configured. Returns true if the domain is parked, false otherwise.
Park a domain by setting up default parking DNS records (A and AAAA records pointing to the parking server). Optionally override existing conflicting records.
Verify that a previously issued nonce still exists and return the canonical SIWE message payload to sign.
Purchase a single domain instantly without adding to cart. Validates domain availability, creates payments and order, then starts the order processing workflow.
Purchase a single domain instantly for trial. Validates domain availability and creates the order, the domain is only registered for a couple of days before it expires or renewed if you please
Purchase a single domain instantly without adding to cart. Validates domain availability, creates payments and order, then starts the order processing workflow.
Purchase a single domain instantly and apply DNS records after processing.
Request NFSC test tokens on Sepolia. Rate limited by user and wallet address.
Park a domain by setting up default parking DNS records (A and AAAA records pointing to the parking server). Optionally override existing conflicting records.
Update an existing DNS record by its ID. Requires domain ownership. The updated record will be validated against DNS zone rules.
Verify a signed SIWE message, consume its nonce, and return a session token plus stored session details.

notes: Prod OpenAPI can lag. Keep prod requestBaseUrl, then safely backfill missing operation discovery metadata from dev and contract.json.
Use live /eip712/* helper endpoints for canonical EIP-712 domain and method types when preparing signatures.
Ignore the published OpenAPI servers array. Use requestBaseUrl from openapi.docs.json instead.
